{{Short description="Quantum field theory of the strong interaction}}
{{Infobox physics
| name = Quantum chromodynamics
| image = 
| caption = 
| topics = [[Strong interaction]], [[Quark]], [[Gluon]], [[Standard Model]], [[Quantum field theory]]
| gauge_group = [[SU(3)]]
| force_carriers = 8 [[gluon]]s
}}

'''Quantum chromodynamics''' ('''QCD''') is the [[quantum field theory]] that describes the [[strong interaction]] — the force that binds [[quark]]s together to form [[hadron]]s such as [[proton]]s and [[neutron]]s. It is a [[non-abelian gauge theory]] with [[SU(3)]] gauge symmetry and is a cornerstone of the [[Standard Model]].

== Colour charge ==
{{Main|Colour charge}}
QCD is based on a property called [[colour charge]] (red, green, blue), analogous to [[electric charge]] in [[quantum electrodynamics]] but with three types instead of one. Each [[quark]] carries a colour charge; each [[gluon]] carries a combination of colour and anti-colour.

== Lagrangian ==
The QCD Lagrangian density:

:<math>\mathcal{L}_{\text{QCD}} = \bar{\psi}_i(i\gamma^\mu(D_\mu)_{ij} - m\delta_{ij})\psi_j - \frac{1}{4}G^a_{\mu\nu}G^{\mu\nu}_a</math>

where:
* <math>\psi_i</math> are quark fields with colour index <math>i = 1, 2, 3</math>
* <math>(D_\mu)_{ij} = \partial_\mu\delta_{ij} - ig_s(T_a)_{ij}A^a_\mu</math> is the covariant derivative
* <math>G^a_{\mu\nu}</math> is the gluon field strength tensor
* <math>T_a</math> are the eight generators of SU(3) ([[Gell-Mann matrices]])
* <math>g_s</math> is the strong coupling constant

== Key features ==

=== Asymptotic freedom ===
At high energies (short distances), the strong coupling constant <math>\alpha_s</math> becomes small:
:<math>\alpha_s(Q^2) \approx \frac{4\pi}{(11 - 2n_f/3)\ln(Q^2/\Lambda_{\text{QCD}}^2)}</math>

Quarks behave as nearly free particles inside hadrons. Discovered by [[David Gross]], [[Frank Wilczek]], and [[David Politzer]] ([[Nobel Prize in Physics|Nobel Prize]] 2004).

=== Colour confinement ===
At low energies (large distances), the force between quarks does not diminish — the potential grows linearly:
:<math>V(r) \approx \sigma r</math>

where <math>\sigma \approx 1\ \text{GeV/fm}</math>. This prevents isolated quarks or gluons from existing; only colour-neutral combinations (hadrons) are observed.

=== Gluon self-interaction ===
Unlike photons in QED, gluons carry colour charge themselves and interact with each other. This is the origin of confinement and asymptotic freedom.

== Experimental evidence ==
* Deep inelastic scattering at [[SLAC]] (1968) — revealed point-like constituents (quarks) in protons
* Three-jet events at [[PETRA]] (1979) — evidence for the gluon
* [[Jet (particle physics)|Jet]] production at high-energy colliders
* Quark–gluon plasma at [[RHIC]] and [[Large Hadron Collider|LHC]]

== Quark–gluon plasma ==
At extreme temperatures (~2×10¹² K), hadrons dissolve into a plasma of free quarks and gluons — the state of matter that existed microseconds after the [[Big Bang]].

== See also ==
{{Portal|Physics}}
* [[Asymptotic freedom]]
* [[Colour charge]]
* [[Gluon]]
* [[Quark]]
* [[Standard Model]]
* [[Strong interaction]]

== References ==
{{Reflist|30em}}

[[Category:Quantum chromodynamics| ]]
[[Category:Quantum field theory]]
[[Category:Strong interaction]]
[[Category:Particle physics]]
